frame_label: Motherhood as Basis of Respect / Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Aajkal log women empowerment ki baat karte hain, and that is good, but real respect tab aata hai jab aadmi samjhe ki har aurat kisi ki maa, beti ya patni hoti hai. Society tabhi strong rehti hai jab hum ghar ki mahilaon ko uss nazar se izzat dein, because they are the ones who hold the family together.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women’s worth and entitlement to respect primarily through their relationships to family roles like mother, daughter, and wife, rather than as individuals. It uses pro-woman language on the surface while encoding relational framing and sacrifice/family-centered value.
original_frame: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
persona_text: You are Suresh. Age 48. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at a dinner party with four other couples. The conversation has drifted to a recent photo of a female celebrity that went viral because she was photographed without makeup and looked very different from her public image. You are giving your opinion on the celebrity and on women who wear so much makeup that they look like a different person and what you think about the honesty of presenting yourself that way and what your wife thinks when she sees you looking at women like that.
